Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: GT65R/GT106-related mannan synthesis-related protein; distantly related to protein O-fucosyltransferases
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Cell wall architecture and hemicellulose deposition
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Knockout analysis; co-expression with CSLAs in yeast; polysaccharide profiling
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enhances (gluco)mannan elongation by CSLAs and can convert mannan synthesis to glucomannan synthesis in certain combinations
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MSR1 is required for full heteromannan biosynthesis in planta. 2. In yeast, co-expression with specific CSLAs alters product identity and boosts glucomannan synthesis. 3. MSR1 alone does not produce mannan, indicating an accessory rather than direct synthase role.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Valuable accessory factor for synthetic biology and pathway engineering to improve mannan yield and tune glucose incorporation
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Biochemical activity remains unresolved; conserved motifs implicated in GDP-sugar binding are required for function.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Functions similarly to MSR1 in planta to support heteromannan biosynthesis
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MSR2 is similar to MSR1 in promoting heteromannan accumulation in Arabidopsis. 2. It acts as a non-catalytic or unresolved accessory factor in mannan synthesis. 3. Its precise biochemical function remains unknown.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate accessory gene for mannan engineering, especially in combination with CSLA enzymes

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports wall mannan biosynthesis by precursor supply
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Protein interaction assay; mutant analysis; polysaccharide profiling
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enhances VTC1 activity and supports GDP-mannose supply; kjc1 mutants severely reduce GGM content in seed mucilage
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. KJC1 physically interacts with and enhances VTC1 activity. 2. Reduced KJC1 function lowers Arabidopsis seed mucilage GGM content. 3. It acts upstream of mannan biosynthesis by controlling nucleotide-sugar precursor production.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to optimize GDP-mannose flux for mannan engineering, with caution due to broader GDP-mannose-dependent pathways
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Despite reduced content, kjc1 mucilage had a relatively normal GGM oligosaccharide profile.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports wall mannan biosynthesis by precursor supply
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Protein interaction evidence referenced in review
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enhances VTC1 activity as a cofactor for GDP-mannose production
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. KJC2 is a KONJAC cofactor associated with VTC1. 2. It contributes to GDP-mannose production upstream of mannan biosynthesis. 3. Direct metabolic effects are less detailed than for KJC1 in this review.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Limited functional detail in the review compared with KJC1.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Cellulose synthase-like A (CSLA), GT2 family, multi-pass membrane glycosyltransferase
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Elongates β-1,4-linked (gluco)mannan backbone using GDP-linked sugar donors
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Cell wall architecture; cellulose interaction; mechanical properties; seed mucilage organization
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Seed mucilage/cell wall; stem cell walls
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in Arabidopsis; associated with seed mucilage-related mannan production
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis; heterologous expression in yeast; in vitro microsome assays; metabolite/polysaccharide profiling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes heteromannan accumulation; csl2 reduces mucilage mannose content and disrupts mucilage architecture
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CSLA2 is a bona fide (gluco)mannan synthase demonstrated in vitro and in vivo. 2. It directly elongates the heteromannan backbone required for Arabidopsis seed mucilage structure. 3. Expression in yeast supports mannan production and compatibility with MSR1 can shift product features.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for engineering mannan-rich biomaterials or dietary fiber traits; can be combined with MSR proteins to tune polymer composition in synthetic biology platforms
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review paper summary; source and applied species are separated because activity was examined both in Arabidopsis and heterologous yeast systems.

Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genetic evidence; heterologous expression attempts; polysaccharide analysis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes to mannan synthesis in stems
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CSLA3 is implicated in (gluco)mannan synthesis in Arabidopsis stems. 2. Its heterologous expression was poor in insect cells, limiting biochemical characterization. 3. It is one of several CSLA enzymes contributing to mannan backbone formation.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for modifying stem hemicellulose composition, though biochemical deployment remains less mature
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No strong heterologous activity data compared with other CSLAs in this review.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Essential for embryo development; cell wall formation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis; heterologous expression; partial rescue by overexpression of other CSLAs
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for normal embryo development; csl7 causes early embryo arrest
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step for embryogenesis-associated mannan synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CSLA7 is a bona fide mannan synthase. 2. Loss of CSLA7 causes embryo-essential defects in Arabidopsis. 3. Its biological role is not fully replaceable by other CSLA paralogs despite partial rescue by CSLA2 or CSLA9 overexpression.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important cautionary target in engineering because some mannan synthases are indispensable for development
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Produces mainly homomannan in heterologous systems and shows lower activity than CSLA2/CSLA9.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Synthesizes β-1,4-mannan in vitro from GDP-mannose and elongates (gluco)mannan backbones
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Cell wall organization and hemicellulose deposition
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Stem cell walls; general cell wall compartment
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: In vitro enzyme assay in microsomes; heterologous expression in yeast; mutant analysis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes mannan biosynthesis; AtCSLA9 specifically used GDP-Man and not UDP-Glc in vitro
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CSLA9 is a bona fide (gluco)mannan synthase. 2. It specifically uses GDP-mannose rather than UDP-glucose in vitro. 3. It contributes to Arabidopsis mannan accumulation and can partially rescue csla7 developmental defects when overexpressed.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful chassis enzyme for heterologous mannan production and mechanistic engineering of donor specificity
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Protease assays suggested the catalytic site faces the microsomal lumen.

Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Polymer decoration step downstream of backbone synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Adds α-1,6-galactose branches to glucomannan acceptors containing repeating Glc-Man disaccharides
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Seed mucilage architecture; cellulose organization; abiotic stress responses
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in seed coat and shoot apical meristem contexts discussed in review
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis; in vitro enzyme assays; microsome expression; GWAS association; complementation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes galactoglucomannan substitution and proper seed mucilage organization; muci10 lowers Gal/Glc/Man in mucilage and alters cellulose architecture
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key polymer-patterning step for mucilage GGM properties
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MUCI10 encodes MAGT1, a strict glucomannan α-galactosyltransferase. 2. It is required for galactoglucomannan structure and normal Arabidopsis seed mucilage architecture. 3. Natural variation in MUC10/MUC110 is linked to mucilage mannose content, and constitutive expression rescues the Ema-1 deficiency.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Salinity mentioned for muci10 seed germination phenotypes
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong target for tuning soluble fiber structure, mucilage properties, and stress-related seed traits through polymer branching control
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper uses both MUCI10 and MUC110 nomenclature in cited studies; core function is galactosylation of glucomannan.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts mannose-1-phosphate and GTP into GDP-mannose
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis; biochemical interaction assays; polysaccharide profiling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduced GDP-mannose synthesis lowers GGM content in Arabidopsis seed mucilage
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VTC1 is the major Arabidopsis pyrophosphorylase producing GDP-mannose. 2. Its activity supports β-mannan biosynthesis as an upstream precursor-supply step. 3. vtc1 mutants have severely reduced GGM content in seed mucilage.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential lever to increase mannan precursor supply, but pleiotropy is expected because GDP-mannose also feeds ascorbate and N-glycosylation pathways
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Likely pleiotropic due to shared GDP-mannose use in ascorbate biosynthesis and protein N-glycosylation

Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Polymer modification step downstream of backbone synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers O-acetyl groups onto mannan backbone residues
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Modulates polymer properties and wall interactions
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MOAT1 is one of four Arabidopsis TBL proteins with mannan O-acetyltransferase activity. 2. It participates in post-synthetic acetylation of mannan polymers. 3. Mannan acetylation likely modulates polymer physicochemical properties.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Low transferase activity in vitro reported for some MOATs.

Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Polymer modification step downstream of backbone synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers O-acetyl groups onto mannan residues
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MOAT2 is among the Arabidopsis TBL proteins acting as mannan O-acetyltransferases. 2. It participates in mannan backbone acetylation. 3. This modification contributes to the chemical diversity of plant heteromannans.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Specific in vivo contribution less detailed in the review.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: TBL family / DUF231 mannan O-acetyltransferase with GDS and DXXH motifs
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Polymer modification step downstream of backbone synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers O-acetyl groups to the C-2 and C-3 positions of mannose residues in mannan acceptors
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Alters wall polymer chemistry and recalcitrance/interaction properties
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: In vitro assays with radiolabeled acetyl-CoA; site-directed mutagenesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Major contributor to mannan O-acetylation with high transferase activity
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MOAT3 is a high-activity mannan O-acetyltransferase. 2. It acetylates mannose residues at C-2 and C-3, matching native Arabidopsis stem glucomannan. 3. Conserved GDS and DXXH motifs are essential for activity.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target to tune biomass recalcitrance and mannan material properties through acetylation control
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Highlighted as one of the strongest biochemically supported MOAT enzymes.

Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Polymer modification step downstream of backbone synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers O-acetyl groups onto mannan residues
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: High acetyltransferase activity contributing to mannan acetylation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MOAT4 is one of the high-activity Arabidopsis mannan O-acetyltransferases. 2. It contributes to post-synthetic acetylation of heteromannan backbones. 3. Together with MOAT3, it supports native acetylation patterns in Arabidopsis stems.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Specific positional preferences less emphasized than for MOAT3.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyzes α-galactosyl side chains from galactomannan-type polysaccharides
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for remodeling/degradation of target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression and in vitro enzyme assay
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Shows α-galactosidase activity in vitro, but in planta role remains unclear
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AGAL2 has in vitro α-galactosidase activity. 2. It is a candidate mannan-debranching enzyme. 3. Its native biological role in Arabidopsis remains unresolved.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review explicitly notes unclear roles in planta.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyzes α-galactosyl side chains from galactomannan-type substrates
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for remodeling/degradation of target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression and in vitro enzyme assay
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Shows α-galactosidase activity in vitro; in planta function unclear
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AGAL3 has α-galactosidase activity in vitro. 2. It is implicated as a potential heteromannan debranching enzyme. 3. Its physiological substrate and role in Arabidopsis are unresolved.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review explicitly notes unclear biological roles.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyzes internal β-1,4-mannosidic bonds in mannan polymers
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Reserve mobilization; cell wall remodeling; possible signaling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Exhibits β-1,4-mannanase activity in vitro, but in planta role is unclear
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MAN1 encodes a β-1,4-mannanase active in vitro. 2. It is a candidate extracellular mannan-remodeling enzyme. 3. Its in vivo role remains unclear.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review notes limited in planta evidence.

Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Reported gene family association; literature citation in review
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Assigned as mannanase-family member affecting mannan remodeling, but detailed role not described here
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MAN2 is listed as a mannan-related hydrolase in Arabidopsis. 2. It likely contributes to extracellular mannan remodeling. 3. Detailed in vivo function is not elaborated in this review.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Evidence is less detailed than for MAN3, MAN5-7.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Hydrolysis of mannan backbone linked to signaling
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyzes β-1,4-mannan polymers, releasing mannose-related signals in the apoplast
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Generates signaling-active degradation products from target polymer
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Cadmium tolerance signaling; defense activation context
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant/forward genetic evidence; in vitro and in vivo activity; exogenous mannose application
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enhances tolerance to cadmium by releasing mannose in the apoplast
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MAN3 is an active β-1,4-mannanase with in vitro and in vivo support. 2. It promotes cadmium tolerance by releasing mannose in the apoplast. 3. Mannose acts in a signaling pathway perceived by a putative receptor-like component.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to improve heavy-metal tolerance through controlled wall-derived sugar signaling
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review highlights signaling roles beyond classical reserve mobilization.
